auto.description: Run LF SEARCH / HF SEARCH / DATA PLOT / DATA SAVE

options.description: Continue searching even after a first hit

clear.description: Clear the Proxmark3 client terminal screen
examples: clear -b                       -> clear the terminal screen and the scrollback buffer

msleep.description: Sleep for given amount of milliseconds

rem.description: Add a text line in log file
examples: rem my message                 -> adds a timestamp with `my message`

prefs_get_barmode.description: Get preference of HF/LF tune command styled output in the client

prefs_get_client.debug.description: Get preference of using clientside debug level

prefs_get_client.delay.description: Get preference of delay time before execution of a command in the client

prefs_get_client.timeout.description: Get preference of delay time before execution of a command in the client

prefs_get_color.description: Get preference of using colors in the client

prefs_get_savepaths.description: Get preference of file paths in the client

prefs_get_emoji.description: Get preference of using emojis in the client

prefs_get_hints.description: Get preference of showing hint messages in the client

prefs_get_output.description: Get preference of dump output style

prefs_get_plotsliders.description: Get preference of showing the plotslider control in the client

prefs_get_mqtt.description: Get preference of MQTT settings in the client

prefs_set_barmode.description: Set persistent preference of HF/LF tune command styled output in the client

options.description: measured values as numbers and bar

prefs_set_client.debug.description: Set persistent preference of using clientside debug level

prefs_set_client.delay.description: Set persistent preference of delay before executing a command in the client
examples: prefs set client.delay --ms 0           -> unsets any delay

prefs_set_client.timeout.description: Set persistent preference of client communication timeout
examples: prefs set client.timeout --ms 0           -> unsets any timeout
prefs set client.timeout -m 20            -> Set the timeout to 20ms
prefs set client.timeout --ms 500         -> Set the timeout to 500ms

prefs_set_color.description: Set persistent preference of using colors in the client

prefs_set_emoji.description: Set persistent preference of using emojis in the client

prefs_set_hints.description: Set persistent preference of showing hint messages in the client

prefs_set_savepaths.description: Set persistent preference of file paths in the client

options.description: create directory if it does not exist

examples: prefs set savepaths --dump /home/mydumpfolder           -> all dump files will be saved into this folder
prefs set savepaths --def /home/myfolder -c         -> create if needed, all files will be saved into this folder

prefs_set_output.description: Set dump output style to condense consecutive repeated data
examples: prefs set output --normal      -> sets the output style to normal
prefs set output --dense       -> sets the output style to dense

prefs_set_plotsliders.description: Set persistent preference of showing the plotslider control in the client

prefs_set_mqtt.description: Set persistent preference MQTT Server in the client

analyse_lrc.description:
Specifying the bytes of a UID with a known LRC will find the last byte value
needed to generate that LRC with a rolling XOR. All bytes should be specified in HEX.

options.description: bytes to calc missing XOR in a LRC

examples: analyse lrc -d 04008064BA          ->  Target (BA) requires final LRC XOR byte value: 5A

analyse_crc.description:
A stub method to test different crc implementations inside the PM3 sourcecode.
Just because you figured out the poly, doesn't mean you get the desired output

analyse_chksum.description:
The bytes will be added with eachother and than limited with the applied mask
Finally compute ones' complement of the least significant bytes.

options.description: bit mask to limit the output (4 hex bytes max)

analyse_dates.description: Tool to look for date/time stamps in a given array of bytes

analyse_lfsr.description: looks at LEGIC Prime's lfsr,  iterates the first 48 values

options.description: lfsr data to find (1 hex byte)

analyse_demodbuff.description: loads a binary string into DemodBuffer

analyse_units.description: experiments of unit conversions found in HF. ETU (1/13.56mhz), US or SSP_CLK (1/3.39MHz)

data_clear.description:
This function clears the BigBuf on device side
and graph window ( graphbuffer )

data_load.description: This command loads the contents of a pm3 file into graph window

options.description: Load data from file without any transformations

data_num.description:
Function takes a decimal or hexdecimal number and print it in decimal/hex/binary
Will print message if number is a prime number

data_plot.description:
Show graph window
hit 'h' in window for detail keystroke help available

data_print.description:
Print the data in the DemodBuffer as hex or binary.
Defaults to binary output

options.description: strip leading zeroes, i.e. set offset to first bit equal to one
output in hex (omit for binary output)

data_save.description:
Save signal trace from graph window , i.e. the GraphBuffer
This is a text file with number -127 to 127.  With the option `w` you can save it as wave file
Filename should be without file extension
examples: data save -f myfilename              -> save graph buffer to file
data save --wave -f myfilename       -> save graph buffer to wave file

data_setdebugmode.description: Set debugging level on client side

data_xor.description:
takes input string and xor string. Perform xor on it.
If no xor string, try the most reoccuring value to xor against

data_biphaserawdecode.description:
Biphase decode binary stream in DemodBuffer
Converts 10 or 01 -> 1 and 11 or 00 -> 0
- must have binary sequence in DemodBuffer (run `data rawdemod --ar` before)
- invert for Conditional Dephase Encoding (CDP) AKA Differential Manchester

options.description: set to adjust decode start position

examples: data biphaserawdecode          -> decode biphase bitstream from the DemodBuffer
data biphaserawdecode -oi      -> decode biphase bitstream from the DemodBuffer, adjust offset, and invert output

data_detectclock.description: Detect ASK, FSK, NRZ, PSK clock rate of wave in GraphBuffer
examples: data detectclock --nzr         -> detect clock of an nrz/direct wave in GraphBuffer

data_fsktonrz.description:
Convert fsk2 to nrz wave for alternate fsk demodulating (for weak fsk)
Omitted values are autodetect instead

data_manrawdecode.description:
Manchester decode binary stream in DemodBuffer
Converts 10 and 01 and converts to 0 and 1 respectively
- must have binary sequence in DemodBuffer (run `data rawdemod --ar` before)

data_modulation.description: search LF signal after clock and modulation

data_rawdemod.description: Demodulate the data in the GraphBuffer and output binary

data_askedgedetect.description:
Adjust Graph for manual ASK demod using the length of sample differences
to detect the edge of a wave

data_autocorr.description:
Autocorrelate over window is used to detect repeating sequences.
We use it as detection of how long in bits a message inside the signal is

data_cthreshold.description: Inverse of dirty threshold command,  all values between up and down will be average out

data_dirthreshold.description: Max rising higher up-thres/ Min falling lower down-thres, keep rest as prev.

data_decimate.description: Performs decimation, by reducing samples N times in the grapbuf. Good for PSK

data_envelope.description: Create an square envelope of the samples

data_grid.description:
This function overlay grid on graph plot window.
use zero value to turn off either

data_getbitstream.description:
Convert GraphBuffer's value accordingly
- larger or equal to ONE becomes ONE
- less than ONE becomes ZERO

data_hpf.description: Remove DC offset from trace. It should centralize around 0

data_iir.description: Apply IIR buttersworth filter on plot data

data_ltrim.description: Trim samples from left of trace
examples: data ltrim -i 300              -> remove from start 0 to index 300

data_mtrim.description:
Trim out samples from
start 0 to `-s index`
AND
from `-e index` to end of graph buffer
examples: data mtrim -s 1000 -e 2000     ->  keep all between index 1000 and 2000

data_rtrim.description: Trim samples from right of trace
examples: data rtrim -i 4000             -> remove from index 4000 to end of graph buffer

data_setgraphmarkers.description: Set the locations of the markers in the graph window

options.description: keep the current values of the markers

examples: data setgraphmarkers -a 64               -> set A, reset the rest
data setgraphmarkers -d --keep           -> set D, keep the rest

data_shiftgraphzero.description: Shift 0 for Graphed wave + or - shift value

data_timescale.description:
Set cursor display timescale.
Setting the timescale makes the differential `dt` reading between the yellow and purple markers meaningful.
once the timescale is set, the differential reading between brackets can become a time duration.

options.description: sets timescale factor according to sampling rate

examples: data timescale --sr 125   -u ms       -> for LF sampled at 125 kHz. Reading will be in milliseconds
data timescale --sr 1.695 -u us       -> for HF sampled at 16 * fc/128. Reading will be in microseconds
data timescale --sr 16    -u ETU      -> for HF with 16 samples per ETU (fc/128). Reading will be in ETUs

data_undecimate.description: Performs un-decimation, by repeating each sample N times in the graphbuf

data_atr.description: look up ATR record from bytearray

data_bitsamples.description: Get raw samples from device as bitstring

data_bmap.description:
Breaks down a hex value to binary according a template
data bmap -d 16 -m 4,4
This will give two rows each with four bits

data_crypto.description:
This command lets you encrypt or decrypt data using DES/3DES/AES.
Supply data, key, IV (needed for des MAC or aes), and cryptography action.

data_diff.description:
Diff takes a multitude of input data and makes a binary compare.
It accepts filenames (filesystem or RDV4 flashmem SPIFFS), emulator memory, magic gen1

data_hexsamples.description: Dump big buffer as hex bytes
examples: data hexsamples -n 128         ->  dumps 128 bytes from offset 0

data_samples.description:
Get raw samples for graph window (GraphBuffer) from device.
If 0, then get whole big buffer from device.

data_qrcode.description: Generate a QR code with the input data

data_test_ss8.description: Tests the implementation of Buffer Save States (8-bit buffer)

data_test_ss32.description: Tests the implementation of Buffer Save States (32-bit buffer)

data_test_ss32s.description: Tests the implementation of Buffer Save States (32-bit signed buffer)

emv_list.description:
Alias of `trace list -t 7816` with selected protocol data to annotate trace buffer
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default
It accepts all other arguments of `trace list`. Note that some might not be relevant for this specific protocol

options.description: display times in microseconds instead of clock cycles
show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

options.flag: or to import into Wireshark using encapsulation type "ISO 14443"

examples: emv list --frame               -> show frame delay times

emv_challenge.description:
Executes Generate Challenge command. It returns 4 or 8-byte random number from card.
Needs a EMV applet to be selected and GPO to be executed.

options.description: Keep field ON for next command
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv challenge -k               -> get challenge, keep filled ON

options.description: Force search AID. Search AID instead of execute PPSE
Transaction type - qVSDC or M/Chip plus CDA (SDAD generation)
Transaction type - VSDC. For test only. Not a standard behavior
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv exec -sat                  -> select card, execute MSD transaction, show APDU and TLV
emv exec -satc                 -> select card, execute CDA transaction, show APDU and TLV

emv_genac.description:
Generate Application Cryptogram command. It returns data in TLV format.
Needs a EMV applet to be selected and GPO to be executed.

options.description: Keep field ON for next command
Executes CDA transaction. Needs to get SDAD in results.
Terminal decision. aac - declined, tc - approved, arqc - online authorisation requested
Load parameters from `emv_defparams.json` file for CDOLdata making from CDOL and parameters
Make CDOLdata from CDOL (tag 8C and 8D) and parameters (def: use default parameters)
TLV decode results of selected applets
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv genac -k 0102              -> generate AC with 2-byte CDOLdata and keep field ON after command
emv genac -t 01020304          -> generate AC with 4-byte CDOL data, show result in TLV
emv genac -Daac 01020304       -> generate AC with 4-byte CDOL data and terminal decision 'declined'
emv genac -pmt 9F 37 04        -> load params from file, make CDOL data from CDOL, generate AC with CDOL, show result in TLV

emv_gpo.description:
Executes Get Processing Options command. It returns data in TLV format (0x77 - format2)
or plain format (0x80 - format1). Needs a EMV applet to be selected.

options.description: Keep field ON for next command
Load parameters from `emv_defparams.json` file for PDOLdata making from PDOL and parameters
Make PDOLdata from PDOL (tag 9F38) and parameters (def: uses default parameters)
TLV decode results of selected applets
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv gpo -t 01020304            -> execute GPO with 4-byte PDOL data, show result in TLV
emv gpo -pmt 9F 37 04          -> load params from file, make PDOL data from PDOL, execute GPO with PDOL, show result in TLV

emv_intauth.description:
Generate Internal Authenticate command. Usually needs 4-byte random number. It returns data in TLV format .
Needs a EMV applet to be selected and GPO to be executed.

options.description: Keep field ON for next command
Load parameters from `emv_defparams.json` file for DDOLdata making from DDOL and parameters
Make DDOLdata from DDOL (tag 9F49) and parameters (def: use default parameters)
TLV decode results of selected applets
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv intauth -k 01020304        -> execute Internal Authenticate with 4-byte DDOLdata and keep field ON after command
emv intauth -t 01020304        -> execute Internal Authenticate with 4-byte DDOL data, show result in TLV
emv intauth -pmt 9F 37 04      -> load params from file, make DDOL data from DDOL, Internal Authenticate with DDOL, show result in TLV

emv_pse.description: Executes PSE/PPSE select command. It returns list of applet on the card:

options.description: Keep field ON for next command
TLV decode results of selected applets
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv pse -st2                   -> select, get ppse, show result in TLV

emv_reader.description:
Act as a EMV reader to identify tag. Look for EMV tags until Enter or the pm3 button is pressed
In `verbose` mode it will also try to extract and decode the transaction logs stored on card in either channel.

options.description: Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

emv_readrec.description:
Executes Read Record command. It returns data in TLV format.
Needs a bank applet to be selected and sometimes needs GPO to be executed.

options.description: Keep field ON for next command
TLV decode results of selected applets
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv readrec -k 0101            -> read file SFI=01, SFIrec=01
emv readrec -kt 0201           -> read file 0201 and show result in TLV

emv_roca.description: Tries to extract public keys and run the ROCA test against them.

options.description: Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv roca -w                    -> select --CONTACT-- card and run test
emv roca                       -> select --CONTACTLESS-- card and run test

emv_scan.description:
Scan EMV card and save it contents to a file.
It executes EMV contactless transaction and saves result to a file which can be used for emulation

options.description: Extract TLV elements and fill Application Data
Transaction type - qVSDC or M/Chip plus CDA (SDAD generation)
Transaction type - VSDC. For test only. Not a standard behavior
Merge output file with card's data. (warning: the file may be corrupted!)
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv scan -at                   -> scan MSD transaction mode and show APDU and TLV
emv scan -c                    -> scan CDA transaction mode

emv_search.description: Tries to select all applets from applet list

options.description: Keep field ON for next command
TLV decode results of selected applets
Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv search -s                  -> select card and search
emv search -st                 -> select card, search and show result in TLV

options.description: Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

examples: emv select -s a00000000101         -> select card, select applet
emv select -st a00000000101        -> select card, select applet, show result in TLV

emv_smart2nfc.description: Executes ISO14443a payment, TX using ISO7816 interface for authentication

options.description: test that the attached card is working (must be VISA)

examples: emv smart2nfc -t               -> test that the attached card is working (must be VISA)

hf_list.description:
Alias of `trace list -t raw` with selected protocol data to annotate trace buffer
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default
It accepts all other arguments of `trace list`. Note that some might not be relevant for this specific protocol

options.description: display times in microseconds instead of clock cycles
show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

options.flag: or to import into Wireshark using encapsulation type "ISO 14443"

examples: hf list --frame                -> show frame delay times

hf_plot.description: Plots HF signal after RF signal path and A/D conversion.

hf_tune.description:
Continuously measure HF antenna tuning.
Press pm3 button or <Enter> to interrupt.

hf_search.description:
Will try to find a HF read out of the unknown tag.
Continues to search for all different HF protocols.

hf_sniff.description:
The high frequency sniffer will assign all available memory on device for sniffed data.
Use `data samples` to download from device and `data plot` to visualize it.
Press button to quit the sniffing.

options.flag: --smode [none|drop|min|max|avg] Skip mode. It switches on the function that applies to several samples before they saved to memory

options.description: Skip ratio. It applied the function above to (ratio * 2) samples. For ratio = 1 it 2 samples.

examples: hf sniff --sp 1000 --st 0      -> skip 1000 pairs, skip 0 triggers

hf_14a_list.description:
Alias of `trace list -t 14a -c` with selected protocol data to annotate trace buffer
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default
It accepts all other arguments of `trace list`. Note that some might not be relevant for this specific protocol

options.description: display times in microseconds instead of clock cycles
show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

options.flag: or to import into Wireshark using encapsulation type "ISO 14443"

examples: hf 14a list --frame            -> show frame delay times

hf_14a_antifuzz.description: Tries to fuzz the ISO14443a anticollision phase

hf_14a_cuids.description: Collect n>0 ISO14443-a UIDs in one go

hf_14a_info.description: This command makes more extensive tests against a ISO14443a tag in order to collect information

options.description: checks if AIDs from aidlist.json is present on the card and prints information about found AIDs

examples: hf 14a info -nsv               -> shows full information about the card

hf_14a_sim.description:
Simulate ISO/IEC 14443 type A tag with 4,7 or 10 byte UID
Use type 7 for Mifare Ultralight EV1, Amiibo (NTAG215 pack 0x8080)

options.description: Exit simulation after <numreads> blocks have been read by reader. 0 = infinite
Performs the 'reader attack', nr/ar attack against a reader
Fill simulator keys from found keys
<8|16> hex bytes ULC/ULAES Auth reply step1: ek(RndB)
<8|16> hex bytes ULC/ULAES Auth reply step2: ek(RndA')

examples: hf 14a sim -t 7                      -> MFU EV1 / NTAG 215 Amiibo

hf_14a_simaid.description:
Simulate ISO/IEC 14443 type A tag with 4,7 or 10 byte UID, and filter for AID Values
These AID Values can be responded to and include extra APDU commands on GetData after response

options.description: <0-30> hex bytes for AID to respond to (Default: A000000000000000000000)

options.flag: -e, --selectaid_response <hex> <0-100> hex bytes for APDU Response to AID Select (Default: 9000)

options.description: <0-100> hex bytes for APDU Response to Get Data request after AID (Default: 9000)
Enumerate all AID values via returning Not Found and print them to console

examples: hf 14a simaid -t 3 --aid a000000000000000000000 --selectaid_response 9000 --getdata_response 9000       -> Custom AID and responses
hf 14a simaid -t 3 --ats 0578817222 --selectaid_response 01009000 --getdata_response 86009000           -> Custom ATS and responses

hf_14a_sniff.description:
Sniff the communication between reader and tag
Use `hf 14a list` to view collected data.

options.description: triggered by first data from card
triggered by first 7-bit request from reader (REQ, WUP)
Console will not be returned until sniff finishes or is aborted

hf_14a_raw.description: Sends raw bytes over ISO14443a. With option to use TOPAZ 14a mode.

options.description: Active signal field ON without select
Keep signal field ON after receive
Active signal field ON with select
Number of bits to send. Useful for send partial byte
Wait in microseconds between select and command
Use Topaz protocol to send command
use secure channel. Must have key

examples: hf 14a raw -sc 3000            -> select, crc, where 3000 == 'read block 00'

hf_14a_reader.description: Act as a ISO-14443a reader to identify tag. Look for ISO-14443a tags until Enter or the pm3 button is pressed

options.description: keep the field active after command executed

hf_14a_apdu.description:
Sends an ISO 7816-4 APDU via ISO 14443-4 block transmission protocol (T=CL).
Works with all APDU types from ISO 7816-4:2013
note:
`-m` and `-d` goes hand in hand
-m <CLA INS P1 P2> -d 325041592E5359532E4444463031
OR
use `-d` with complete APDU data
-d 00A404000E325041592E5359532E444446303100

options.description: keep signal field ON after receive
APDU header, 4 bytes <CLA INS P1 P2>
make extended length apdu if `m` parameter included
Le APDU parameter if `m` parameter included
full APDU package or data if `m` parameter included

hf_14a_apdufind.description:
Enumerate APDU's of ISO7816 protocol to find valid CLS/INS/P1/P2 commands.
It loops all 256 possible values for each byte.
The loop oder is INS -> P1/P2 (alternating) -> CLA.
Tag must be on antenna before running.

options.description: Start value of CLASS (1 hex byte)
Start value of INSTRUCTION (1 hex byte)
Start value of P1 (1 hex byte)
Start value of P2 (1 hex byte)
Minimum secondes before resetting the tag (to prevent timeout issues). Default is 5 minutes
Maximum times an status word other than 0x9000 or 0x6D00 is shown. Default is 512.
Do not test an instruction (can be specified multiple times)
Search  for APDUs with Le=0 (case 2S) as well

hf_14a_chaining.description: Enable/Disable ISO14443a input chaining. Maximum input length goes from ATS.
examples: hf 14a chaining                -> show chaining enable/disable state

hf_14a_ndefformat.description: Format ISO14443-a Tag as a NFC tag with Data Exchange Format (NDEF)

hf_14a_ndefread.description: Read NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF) file on Type 4 NDEF tag
examples: hf 14a ndefread -f myfilename      -> save raw NDEF to file

hf_14a_ndefwrite.description: Write raw NDEF hex bytes to tag. This commands assumes tag already been NFC/NDEF formatted.

options.description: write raw NDEF file to tag
fix NDEF record headers / terminator block if missing

examples: hf 14a ndefwrite -d 0300FE           -> write empty record to tag

hf_14b_list.description:
Alias of `trace list -t 14b -c` with selected protocol data to annotate trace buffer
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default
It accepts all other arguments of `trace list`. Note that some might not be relevant for this specific protocol

options.description: display times in microseconds instead of clock cycles
show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

options.flag: or to import into Wireshark using encapsulation type "ISO 14443"

examples: hf 14b list --frame            -> show frame delay times

hf_14b_apdu.description:
Sends an ISO 7816-4 APDU via ISO 14443-4 block transmission protocol (T=CL).
works with all apdu types from ISO 7816-4:2013

options.description: leave the signal field ON after receive response
executes TLV decoder if it possible
decode apdu request if it possible
make apdu with head from this field and data from data field.

options.flag: must be 4 bytes: <CLA INS P1 P2>

options.description: make extended length apdu if `m` parameter included
Le apdu parameter if `m` parameter included

hf_14b_dump.description:
This command dumps the contents of a ISO-14443-B tag and save it to file
Tries to autodetect cardtype, memory size defaults to SRI4K

options.description: (optional) filename,  if no <name> UID will be used as filename

hf_14b_info.description: Tag information for ISO/IEC 14443 type B based tags

options.description: checks if AIDs from aidlist.json is present on the card and prints information about found AIDs

examples: hf 14b ndefread -f myfilename        -> save raw NDEF to file

hf_14b_raw.description: Sends raw bytes to card. Activates field by default

options.description: active signal field ON without select
leave the signal field ON after receive response
do not read response from card

hf_14b_reader.description: Act as a 14443B reader to identify a tag

options.description: show anticollision signal trace in plot window

hf_14b_restore.description:
Restore data from (bin/eml/json) dump file to tag
If the dump file includes the special block at the end it will be ignored

options.description: (optional) filename,  if no <name> UID will be used as filename

hf_14b_sim.description: Simulate a ISO/IEC 14443 type B tag with 4 byte UID / PUPI

hf_14b_sniff.description:
Sniff the communication between reader and tag
Use `hf 14b list` to view collected data.

hf_14b_wrbl.description:
Write data to a SRI512 or SRIX4K block
If writing to a block out-of-range,  use `--force` to override checks
Special block at end denots OTP and lock bits among others

options.description: special block write at end of memory (0xFF)

hf_14b_tearoff.description:
Use tear-off technique to manipulate ST25TB/SRx monotonic counter blocks.
This exploits EEPROM tearing to increment counters that normally can only
be decremented. Based on the near-field-chaos project by SecLabz.
The attack works by sending a write command and cutting the RF field at
a precise moment, causing a partial write that can raise the counter value.
The operation usually takes a few seconds to a few minutes.
NOTE: 0xFFFFFFFE values may be unstable due to tag internals.
Keep the tag positioned steadily on the antenna.

options.description: block number (typically 5 or 6 for ST25TB counters)
target counter value (4 hex bytes, e.g. FFFFFFFE)

hf_14b_view.description:
Print a ISO14443-B dump file (bin/eml/json)
note:
- command expects the filename to contain a UID
which is needed to determine card memory type

options.description: Specify a filename for dump file

hf_14b_calypso.description: Reads out the contents of a ISO14443B Calypso card

hf_14b_mobib.description: Reads out the contents of a ISO14443B Mobib card

hf_14b_setuid.description: Set UID for magic card (only works with such cards)

hf_15_list.description:
Alias of `trace list -t 15 -c` with selected protocol data to annotate trace buffer
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default
It accepts all other arguments of `trace list`. Note that some might not be relevant for this specific protocol

show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

hf_15_demod.description:
Tries to demodulate / decode ISO-15693, from downloaded samples.
Gather samples with 'hf 15 samples' / 'hf 15 sniff'

hf_15_dump.description: This command dumps the contents of a ISO-15693 tag and save to file (bin/json)

options.description: Specify a filename for dump file

hf_15_info.description: Uses the optional command `get_systeminfo` 0x2B to try and extract information

options.flag: -k, --k-persistent, --key-persistent, --kpersistent, --keypersistent, --kp <hex> Kpersistent (32 bytes, optional; used for fast cryptogram verification)

-p, --reader-private-key, --readerprivkey, --rpk <pem|der-b64|der-hex|scalar-b64|scalar-hex|path> Reader private key (P-256): PEM, DER hex, scalar hex/base64, or file path

options.description: Top level file (or application) ID (FID) 2 bytes
Child file ID (EF under application/master file) 2 bytes

hf cipurse read --aid 4144204631 --fid 0102         -> read file with id 0102 from application 4144204631

hf_cipurse_write.description: Write file in the application by file ID with key ID and key. If no key is supplied, default key of 737373...7373 will be used

Data to write to new file

hf cipurse write --aid 4144204631 --fid 0102 -d aabb       -> write file with id 0102 in the 4144204631 application

hf cipurse aread --chfid 0102       -> read file 0102 attributes in the default application
hf cipurse aread --aid 4144204632 --chfid 0102       -> read file 0102 attributes in the 4144204632 application
hf cipurse aread -n 2 -k 65656565656565656565656565656565 --fid 2ff7      -> Authenticate keyID 2, read file attributes

hf_cipurse_awrite.description: Write file attributes by file ID with key ID and key. If no key is supplied, default key of 737373...7373 will be used

options.description: Child file ID (EF under application/master file) ( 2 bytes )

examples: hf cipurse awrite --fid 2ff7 -d 080000C1C1C1C1C1C1C1C1C1         -> write default file attributes with id 2ff7
hf cipurse awrite --mfd -d 080000FFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF86023232 --commit         -> write file attributes for master file (MF)
hf cipurse awrite --chfid 0102 -d 020000ffffff       -> write file 0102 attributes in the default application to full access
hf cipurse awrite --chfid 0102 -d 02000040ffff       -> write file 0102 attributes in the default application to full access with keys 1 and 2

hf_cipurse_updkey.usage:
hf cipurse updkey [-hav] [-n <dec>] [-k <hex>] [--aid <hex 1..16 bytes>] [--fid <hex 2 bytes>] [--mfd]
[--newkeyn <dec>] [--newkey <hex 16 byte>] [--newkeya <hex 1 byte>] [--enckeyn <dec>]

[--no-auth] [--commit]

Encrypt key (must be equal to the key on the card)

hf_cipurse_updakey.description:
Update key attributes. Factory default - 0x02.
b0 - Update right - 1 self
b1 - Change key and rights - 0 frozen
b2 - Use as key encryption key - 1 blocked
b8 - Key validity - 0 valid

hf cipurse updakey [-hav] [-n <dec>] [-k <hex>] [--aid <hex 1..16 bytes>] [--fid <hex 2 bytes>] [--mfd]

[--sresp <plain|mac(default)|encode>] [--no-auth] [--commit]
examples: hf cipurse updakey --trgkeyn 2 --attr 80      ->  block key 2 for lifetime (WARNING!)

- Unsuccessful == Status Flag1 and Flag2

- Un-/Ssuccessful == Status Flag1 and Flag2

Dump all existing Area Code and Service Code.
Only works on services that do not require authentication yet.

hf_felica_discnodes.description:
Dump all existing Area Code and Service Code.
Method: auto | request_code_list | search_service_code | request_service | read_without_encryption

hf_felica_rqservice.description:
Use this command to verify the existence of Area and Service, and to acquire Key Version:
- When the specified Area or Service exists, the card returns Key Version.
- When the specified Area or Service does not exist, the card returns FFFFh as Key Version.
For Node Code List of a command packet, Area Code or Service Code of the target
of acquisition of Key Version shall be enumerated in Little Endian format.
If Key Version of System is the target of acquisition, FFFFh shall be specified
in the command packet.

options.description: auto node number mode, iterates through all nodes 1 < n < 32

hf_felica_rqresponse.description:
Use this command to verify the existence of a card and its Mode.
- current mode of the card is returned

hf_felica_scsvcode.description: Dump all existing Area Code and Service Code.

hf_felica_rqsyscode.description:
Use this command to acquire System Code registered to the card.  - if a card is divided into more than one System,
this command acquires System Code of each System existing in the card.

hf_felica_auth1.description:
Initiate mutual authentication. This command must always be executed before Auth2 command
and mutual authentication is achieve only after Auth2 command has succeeded.
INCOMPLETE / EXPERIMENTAL COMMAND!!!

hf_felica_auth2.description:
Complete mutual authentication. This command can only be executed subsquent to Auth1
INCOMPLETE / EXPERIMENTAL COMMAND!!!
EXPERIMENTAL COMMAND - M2c/P2c will be not checked

hf_felica_rqspecver.description:
Use this command to acquire the version of card OS.
Response:
- Format version: Fixed value 00h. Provided only if Status Flag1 = 00h
- Basic version: Each value of version is expressed in BCD notation. Provided only if Status Flag1 = 00h
- Number of Option: number of entries in Option Version List.
- Option version list: BCD notation (major.minor.patch), little-endian, provided only if Status Flag1 = 00h

Initiate a U2F token registration. Needs two 32-byte hash numbers.
challenge parameter (32b) and application parameter (32b).
The default config filename is  `fido2_defparams.json`
note:
`-vv` shows  full certificates data

options.description: Show DER certificate contents in TLV representation
JSON input file name for parameters

examples: hf fido reg                        -> execute command with 2 parameters, filled 0x00
hf fido reg --cp s0 --ap s1        -> execute command with plain parameters
hf fido reg -f fido2-params        -> execute command with custom config file

hf_fido_auth.description:
Initiate a U2F token authentication. Needs key handle and two 32-byte hash numbers.
key handle(var 0..255), challenge parameter (32b) and application parameter (32b)
The default config filename is  `fido2_defparams.json`
examples: hf fido auth --kh 000102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f000102030405060708090a0b0c0d0e0f      -> execute command with 2 parameters, filled 0x00 and key handle

- for yubikey there must be only one option `"rk": true` or false
note:
`-vv` shows  full certificates data

options.description: Show DER certificate contents in TLV representation

examples: hf fido make                   -> use default parameters file `fido2_defparams.json`

Execute a FIDO2 Get Assertion command. Needs json file with parameters.
Sample file `fido2_defparams.json` in `client/resources/`.
- Needs if `rk` option is `false` (authenticator doesn't store credential to its memory)
- for yubikey there must be only one option `"up": true` or false
note:
`-vv` shows  full certificates data

The first two bytes of the epurse are the debit value (big endian) and may be any value except FFFF.

Make sure you know the target card credit key. Typical  `--ki 1` or `--ki 3`

data to write as 8 hex bytes
key is assumed to be the credit key
use shallow (ASK) reader modulation instead of OOK
tearoff delay start (in us) must be between 1 and 43000 (43ms). Precision is about 1/3 us
tearoff delay end (in us) must be a higher value than the start delay
number of times to loop per tearoff time
Runs the commands on device side and tries to stabilize tears

hf_iclass_loclass.description:
Execute the offline part of loclass attack
An iclass dumpfile is assumed to consist of an arbitrary number of
malicious CSNs, and their protocol responses
The binary format of the file is expected to be as follows:
<8 byte CSN><8 byte CC><4 byte NR><4 byte MAC>
<8 byte CSN><8 byte CC><4 byte NR><4 byte MAC>
<8 byte CSN><8 byte CC><4 byte NR><4 byte MAC>
... totalling N*24 bytes

options.description: filename with nr/mac data from `hf iclass sim -t 2`
Perform self test, including long ones

hf_iclass_lookup.description: This command take sniffed trace data and try to recovery a iCLASS Standard or iCLASS Elite key.

options.description: Dictionary file with default iclass keys
use the VB6 rng for elite keys instead of a dictionary file

hf_iclass_legrec.description:
Attempts to recover the diversified key of a specific iCLASS card. This may take several days.
The card must remain be on the PM3 antenna during the whole process.
! Warning !  This process may brick the card!  ! Warning !

options.description: Where to start from to retrieve the key (def: 0)
The number of key retrieval cycles to perform, max 10000 (def 100)
Re-enables tracing for debugging. Limits cycles to 1
Perform real writes on the card
Loops the loop for 10 times, recommended loop value of 5000
Increases the speed (4.6->7.4 key updates/second), higher risk to brick the card
Lower card comms delay times, further speeds increases, may cause more errors
Estimates the key updates based on the card's CSN assuming standard key, can be used with --credit option
EXPERIMENTAL : Recover the credit key using KD 0

hf_iclass_legbrute.description:
This command takes sniffed trace data and a partial raw key and bruteforces the remaining 40 bits of the raw key.
Complete 40 bit keyspace is 1'099'511'627'776 and command is locked down to max 16 threads currently.
A possible worst case scenario on 16 threads estimates XXX days YYY hours MMM minutes.

options.description: MACs captured from the reader, different than the first set (with the same csn and epurse value)
Partial Key from legrec or starting key of keyblock from legbrute
Where to start from to retrieve the key, default 0 - value in millions e.g. 1 is 1 million
Number of threads to use, by default it uses the cpu's max threads (max 16).

examples: hf iclass legbrute --epurse feffffffffffffff --macs1 1306cad9b6c24466 --macs2 f0bf905e35f97923 --pk B4F12AADC5301225

hf_iclass_unhash.description:
Reverses the hash0 function used generate iclass diversified keys after DES encryption,
Function returns the DES crypted CSN.  Next step bruteforcing.

hf_iclass_blacktears.description:
Tear off the iCLASS (new-silicon only) configuration block to set non-secure page mode.
Make sure you know the target card credit key. Typical  `--ki 1` or `--ki 3`

options.description: Key index to select key from memory 'hf iclass managekeys'
key is assumed to be the credit key
tearoff delay start (in us) must be between 1 and 43000 (43ms). Precision is about 1/3 us
tearoff delay end (in us) must be a higher value than the start delay
Custom OTP value as 2 hex bytes
use shallow (ASK) reader modulation instead of OOK

examples: hf iclass blacktears -k 001122334455667B    <--  debit custom key
hf iclass blacktears --credit --ki 1        <-- credit key at index 1

options.description: Specify CSN as 8 hex bytes to use with sim type 0

examples: hf iclass sim -t 0 --csn 031FEC8AF7FF12E0         -> simulate with specified CSN
hf iclass sim -t 1                                -> simulate with default CSN
hf iclass sim -t 2                                -> execute loclass attack online part
hf iclass sim -t 3                                -> simulate full iCLASS 2k tag
hf iclass sim -t 4                                -> Reader-attack, adapted for KeyRoll mode, gather reader responses to extract elite key
hf iclass sim -t 6                                -> simulate full iCLASS 2k tag that doesn't respond to r/w requests to the last SIO block
hf iclass sim -t 7                                -> simulate full iCLASS 2k tag that doesn't XOR or respond to r/w requests on block 3

hf iclass calcnewkey --old 1122334455667788 --new 2233445566778899                                      -> std to std read csn

hf_iclass_encode.description:
Encode binary wiegand to block 7,8,9
Use either --bin or --wiegand/--fc/--cn
When using emulator you have to first load a credential into emulator memory

options.description: Key index to select key from memory 'hf iclass managekeys'
key is assumed to be the credit key
Write to emulation memory instead of card
use shallow (ASK) reader modulation instead of OOK

examples: hf iclass encode -w H10301 --fc 31 --cn 337 --ki 0                       -> FC 31 CN 337 (H10301)
hf iclass encode --bin 10001111100000001010100011 --ki 0 --elite         -> FC 31 CN 337 (H10301), writing w elite key
hf iclass encode -w H10301 --fc 31 --cn 337 --emu                        -> Writes the ecoded data to emulator memory

hf_iclass_encrypt.description:
3DES encrypt data
OBS! In order to use this function, the file 'iclass_decryptionkey.bin' must reside
in the resources directory. The file should be 16 hex bytes of binary data

hf_iclass_decrypt.description:
3DES decrypt data
This is a naive implementation, it tries to decrypt every block after block 6.
Correct behaviour would be to decrypt only the application areas where the key is valid,
which is defined by the configuration block.
OBS!
In order to use this function, the file `iclass_decryptionkey.bin` must reside
in the resources directory. The file must be 16 bytes binary data
or...
make sure your cardhelper is placed in the sim module

options.description: Specify a filename for dump file

Nested attack for hardened MIFARE Classic cards.
if card is EV1, command can detect and use known key see example below
`--i<X>`  set type of SIMD instructions. Without this flag programs autodetect it.
or
hf mf hardnested -r --tk [known target key]
Add the known target key to check if it is present in the remaining key space
hf mf hardnested --blk 0 -a -k A0A1A2A3A4A5 --tblk 4 --ta --tk FFFFFFFFFFFF

options.description: Read `hf-mf-<UID>-nonces.bin` if tag present, otherwise `nonces.bin`, and start attack
Slower acquisition (required by some non standard cards)

examples: hf mf hardnested --tblk 4 --ta           -> works for MFC EV1

hf_mf_autopwn.description:
This command automates the key recovery process on MIFARE Classic cards.
It uses the fchk, chk, darkside, nested, hardnested and staticnested to recover keys.
If all keys are found, it try dumping card content both to file and emulator memory.
default file name template is `hf-mf-<uid>-<dump|key>.`
using suffix the template becomes `hf-mf-<uid>-<dump|key>-<suffix>.`

options.description: Add this suffix to generated files
Slower acquisition (required by some non standard cards)
legacy mode (use the slow `hf mf chk`)

examples: hf mf autopwn -s 0 -a -k FFFFFFFFFFFF           -> target MFC 1K card, Sector 0 with known key A 'FFFFFFFFFFFF'
hf mf autopwn --1k -f mfc_default_keys          -> target MFC 1K card, default dictionary
hf mf autopwn --1k -s 0 -a -k FFFFFFFFFFFF -f mfc_default_keys        -> combo of the two above samples
hf mf autopwn --1k -s 0 -a -k FFFFFFFFFFFF -k a0a1a2a3a4a5            -> multiple user supplied keys

hf_mf_restore.description:
Restore MIFARE Classic dump file to tag.
The key file and dump file will program the card sector trailers.
By default we authenticate to card with key 0xFFFFFFFFFFFF.
If access rights in dump file is all zeros,  it will be replaced with default values
`--uid` param is used for filename templates `hf-mf-<uid>-dump.bin` and `hf-mf-<uid>-key.bin.
if not specified, it will read the card uid instead.
`--ka` param you can indicate that the key file should be used for authentication instead.
if so we also try both B/A keys
`--force` param is used to override warnings and allow bad ACL block writes.
if not specified, it will skip blocks with bad ACL.

options.description: specify a filename for dump file

New acc rights...  FF 07 80
New GPB..........  69

hf_mf_wrbl.description:
Write MIFARE Classic block with 16 hex bytes of data
Sector 0 / Block 0 - Manufacturer block
When writing to block 0 you must use a VALID block 0 data (UID, BCC, SAK, ATQA)
Writing an invalid block 0 means rendering your Magic GEN2 card undetectable.
Look in the magic_cards_notes.md file for help to resolve it.
`--force` param is used to override warnings like bad ACL and BLOCK 0 writes.
if not specified, it will exit if detected

options.description: input key type is key A (def)

from emulator memory.  See `hf mf eload` first.

hf_ntag424_changefs.description:
Updates file settings for file, must be authenticated.
This is a short explanation of the settings. See AN12196 for more information:
options: byte with bit flags
Bit:   Setting:
6     Enable SDM and mirroring
access: two byte access rights.
Each nibble is a key number, or E for free access.
Order is key for readwrite, change, read and write
sdmoptions: byte with bit flags
Bit:   Setting:
0     ASCII encoding
4     SDMEncFileData
5     SDMReadCtrLimit
6     SDMReadCtr
7     SDMOptionsUID
sdmaccess: two byte access rights.
Each nibble is a key, or E for plain mirror and F for no mirroring
Order is Reserved, SDMCtrRet, SDMMetaRead and SDMFileRead
sdm_data: Three bytes of data used to control SDM settings. Can be specified multiple times.
Data means different things depending on settings.
Note: Not all of these settings will be written. It depends on the option byte, and the keys set. See AN12196 for more information.
You must also start with sdmdata1, then sdmdata2, up to the number of sdm_data you want to write

options.flag: Each nibble is a key number, or E for free access.
Order is key for readwrite, change, read and write
Each nibble is a key, or E for plain mirror and F for no mirroring
Order is Reserved, SDMCtrRet, SDMMetaRead and SDMFileRead
sdm_data: Three bytes of data used to control SDM settings. Can be specified multiple times.
Data means different things depending on settings.
Note: Not all of these settings will be written. It depends on the option byte, and the keys set. See AN12196 for more information.
You must also start with sdmdata1, then sdmdata2, up to the number of sdm_data you want to write

hw_tearoff.description:
Configure a tear-off hook for the next write command supporting tear-off
After having been triggered by a write command, the tear-off hook is deactivated
Delay (in us) must be between 1 and 43000 (43ms). Precision is about 1/3us.

options.description: Delay in us before triggering tear-off, must be between 1 and 43000
Skip N triggers before activating the hook

examples: hw tearoff --on                -> (re)activate a previously defined delay
hw tearoff --off               -> deactivate a previously activated but not yet triggered hook
hw tearoff --list              -> list commands implementing tear-off hooks

Baudrate here is only for physical UART or UART-BT, NOT for USB-CDC or blue shark add-on

options.description: Serial port to connect to, else retry the last used one

hw_dbg.description:
Set device side debug level output.

introducing delays in time critical functions like simulation or sniffing

hw dbg -1                      -> set log level to _error_

hw_fpgaoff.description: Turn of fpga and antenna field

hw_tune.description:
Measure tuning of device antenna. Results shown in graph window.
This command doesn't actively tune your antennas,
it's only informative by measuring voltage that the antennas will generate

hw_decay.description:
Measure HF antenna decay after field-off.
Captures how quickly the peak-detect capacitor voltage drops
after the 13.56 MHz field is turned off. Different antenna loading
(unloaded, booster board, damaged) produces different decay profiles.
examples: hw decay --ms 100              -> stabilize for 100ms before measurement

lf_config.description:
Get/Set config for LF sampling, bit/sample, decimation, frequency
These changes are temporary, will be reset after a power cycle.
- use `lf read` performs a read (active field)
- use `lf sniff` performs a sniff (no active field)

options.description: averaging - if set, will average the stored sample value when decimating (default 1)
sets resolution of bits per sample (default 8)
sets decimation. A value of N saves only 1 in N samples (default 1)
Manually set freq divisor. 88 -> 134 kHz, 95 -> 125 kHz
sets a number of samples to skip before capture (default 0)
sets trigger threshold. 0 means no threshold

examples: lf config -b 8 --125               -> samples at 125 kHz, 8 bps
lf config -b 4 --134 --dec 3       -> samples at 134 kHz, averages three samples into one, stored with a resolution of 4 bits per sample
lf config --trig 20 -s 10000       -> trigger sampling when above 20, skip 10 000 first samples after triggered
lf config --reset                  -> reset back to default values

lf_cmdread.description:
Modulate LF reader field to send command before read. All periods in microseconds.
- use `lf config` to set parameters

options.description: delay OFF period, (0 for bitbang mode)
Extra symbol definition and duration (up to 4)
keep signal field ON after receive
calculate and append CRC-8/HITAG (also for ZX8211)

examples: lf cmdread -d 50 -z 116 -o 166 -e W3000 -c W00110                                 -> probing for Hitag 1/S
lf cmdread -d 50 -z 116 -o 166 -e W3000 -c W11000                                 -> probing for Hitag 2/S
lf cmdread -d 50 -z 116 -o 166 -e W3000 -c W11010                                 -> probing for Hitag S
lf cmdread -d 50 -z 116 -o 166 -e W3000 -c W11000 -s 2000 -@                      -> probing for Hitag 2/S, oscilloscope style
lf cmdread -d 48 -z 112 -o 176 -e W3000 -e S240 -e E336 -c W0S00000010000E        -> probing for Hitag µ(micro)

lf_read.description:
Sniff low frequency signal.
- use `lf config` to set parameters.
- use `data plot` to look at it.
If the number of samples is more than the device memory limit (40000 now), it will try to use the real-time sampling mode.

lf_search.description: Read and search for valid known tag. For offline mode, you can `data load` first then search.

options.description: Use data from Graphbuffer to search (offline mode)

examples: lf search                      -> try reading data from tag & search for known tag
lf search -u                   -> try reading data from tag & search for known and unknown tag
lf search -1                   -> use data from the GraphBuffer & search for known tag
lf search -1uc                 -> use data from the GraphBuffer & search for known and unknown tag

lf_sim.description:
Simulate low frequency tag from graphbuffer
Use `lf config` to set parameters
examples: lf sim --gap 240               -> start simulating with 240ms gap

lf_simask.description: Simulate ASK tag from DemodBuffer or input

options.description: manually set clock - can autodetect if using DemodBuffer (default 64)
add t55xx Sequence Terminator gap - default: no gaps (only manchester)
data to sim - omit to use DemodBuffer

examples: lf simask --clk 64 --am -d ffbd8001686f1924                     -> simulate a EM410x tag
lf simask --clk 64 --am --stt -d 5649533200003F340000001B       -> simulate a VISA2K tag

lf_simfsk.description:
Simulate FSK tag from DemodBuffer or input. There are about four FSK modulations to know of.
FSK1  -  where fc/8 = high  and fc/5 = low
FSK1a -  is inverted FSK1,  ie:   fc/5 = high and fc/8 = low
FSK2  -  where fc/10 = high  and fc/8 = low
FSK2a -  is inverted FSK2,  ie:   fc/10 = high and fc/8 = low
NOTE: if you set one clock manually set them all manually

options.description: manually set clock - can autodetect if using DemodBuffer (default 64)
TBD! - STT to enable a gap between playback repetitions (default: no gap)
data to sim - omit to use DemodBuffer

examples: lf simfsk -c 50 --high 10 --low 8 -d 1D5559555569A9A555A59569              -> simulate HID Prox tag manually
lf simfsk -c 50 --high 10 --low 8 --stt -d 011DB2487E8D811111111111        -> simulate AWID tag manually

lf_simpsk.description: Simulate PSK tag from DemodBuffer or input

options.description: manually set clock - can autodetect if using DemodBuffer (default 32)
data to sim - omit to use DemodBuffer

examples: lf simpsk -1 --clk 40 --fc 4 -d 01020304         -> simulate PSK1 rf/40 psksub fc/4, data 01020304
lf simpsk -1 --clk 32 --fc 2 -d a0000000bd989a11         -> simulate a indala tag manually

lf_simbidir.description: Simulate LF tag with bidirectional data transmission between reader and tag

lf_sniff.description:
Sniff low frequency signal. You need to configure the LF part on the Proxmark3 device manually.
Usually a trigger and skip samples is a good thing to set before doing a low frequency sniff.
- use `lf config` to set parameters.
- use `data plot` to look at sniff signal.
- use `lf search -1` to see if signal can be automatic decoded.
If the number of samples is more than the device memory limit (40000 now), it will try to use the real-time sampling mode.

lf_em_4x70_brute.description:
Optimized partial key-update attack of 16-bit key block 7, 8 or 9 of an EM4x70
This attack does NOT write anything to the tag.
Before starting this attack, 0000 must be written to the 16-bit key block: 'lf em 4x70 write -b 9 -d 0000'.
After success, the 16-bit key block have to be restored with the key found: 'lf em 4x70 write -b 9 -d c0de'

options.description: Start bruteforce enumeration from this key value

examples: lf em 4x70 brute -b 9 --rnd 45F54ADA252AAC --frn 4866BB70          -> bruteforcing key bits k95...k80 (pm3 test key)
lf em 4x70 brute -b 8 --rnd 3FFE1FB6CC513F --frn F355F1A0          -> bruteforcing key bits k79...k64 (research paper key)
lf em 4x70 brute -b 7 --rnd 7D5167003571F8 --frn 982DBCC0          -> bruteforcing key bits k63...k48 (autorecovery test key)

lf_em_4x70_info.description:
Tag Information EM4x70
Tag variants include ID48 automotive transponder.
ID48 does not use command parity (default).
V4070 and EM4170 do require parity bit.

examples: lf em 4x70 write -b 15 -d c0de            -> write 'c0de' to block 15

lf_em_4x70_unlock.description:
Unlock EM4x70 by sending PIN
Default pin may be:
AAAAAAAA
00000000
examples: lf em 4x70 unlock -p 11223344      -> Unlock with PIN

lf_em_4x70_auth.description:
Authenticate against an EM4x70 by sending random number (RN) and F(RN)
If F(RN) is incorrect based on the tag key, the tag will not respond
If F(RN) is correct based on the tag key, the tag will give a 20-bit response
examples: lf em 4x70 auth --rnd 45F54ADA252AAC --frn 4866BB70           -> (using pm3 test key)
lf em 4x70 auth --rnd 3FFE1FB6CC513F --frn F355F1A0           -> (using research paper key)
lf em 4x70 setpin -p 11223344      -> Write new PIN
lf em 4x70 setkey -k F32AA98CF5BE4ADFA6D3480B   (pm3 test key)
lf em 4x70 setkey -k A090A0A02080000000000000   (research paper key)

lf_em_4x70_calc.description: Calculates both the reader and tag challenge for a user-provided key and rnd.

options.description: 56-bit random value sent to tag for authentication

examples: lf em 4x70 calc --key F32AA98CF5BE4ADFA6D3480B --rnd 45F54ADA252AAC   (pm3 test key)
lf em 4x70 calc --key A090A0A02080000000000000 --rnd 3FFE1FB6CC513F   (research paper key)

lf_em_4x70_recover.description:
After obtaining key bits 95..48 (such as via 'lf em 4x70 brute'), this command will recover
key bits 47..00.  By default, this process does NOT require a tag to be present.
By default, the potential keys are shown (typically 1-6) along with a corresponding
'lf em 4x70 auth' command that will authenticate, if that potential key is correct.
The user can copy/paste these commands when the tag is present to manually check
which of the potential keys is correct.
examples: lf em 4x70 recover --key F32AA98CF5BE --rnd 45F54ADA252AAC --frn 4866BB70 --grn 9BD180   (pm3 test key)
lf em 4x70 recover --key A090A0A02080 --rnd 3FFE1FB6CC513F --frn F355F1A0 --grn 609D60   (research paper key)

lf_em_4x70_autorecover.description:
This command will perform automatic recovery of the key from a writable tag.
All steps are possible to do manually.  The corresponding sequence, if done
manually, is as follows:
1. Verify passed parameters authenticate with the tag (safety check)
lf em 4x70 auth --rnd <rnd_1> --frn <frn_1>
2. Brute force the key bits in block 9
lf em 4x70 write -b 9 -d 0000
lf em 4x70 recover -b 9 --rnd <rnd_1> --frn <frn_1>
lf em 4x70 write -b 9 -d <key_block_9>
3. Brute force the key bits in block 8
lf em 4x70 write -b 8 -d 0000
lf em 4x70 recover -b 8 --rnd <rnd_1> --frn <frn_1>
lf em 4x70 write -b 8 -d <key_block_8>
4. Brute force the key bits in block 7
lf em 4x70 write -b 7 -d 0000)
lf em 4x70 recover -b 7 --rnd <rnd_1> --frn <frn_1>
lf em 4x70 write -b 7 -d <key_block_7>
5. Recover potential values of the lower 48 bits of the key
lf em 4x70 recover --key <key_block_9><key_block_8><key_block_7> --rnd <rnd_1> --frn <frn_1>
6. Verify which potential key is actually on the tag (using a different rnd/frn combination)
lf em 4x70 auth --rnd <rnd_2> --frn <frn_N>
7. Print the validated key
This command simply requires the rnd/frn/grn from a single known-good authentication.

options.description: F(RN) 28-bit as 4 hex bytes from known-good authentication
G(RN) 20-bit as 3 hex bytes from known-good authentication

examples: lf em 4x70 autorecover --rnd 3FFE1FB6CC513F --frn F355F1A0 --grn 609D60   (research paper key)

lf_hid_brute.description:
Enables bruteforce of HID readers with specified facility code or card number. This is an attack against the reader.
If the field being bruteforced is provided, it starts with it and goes up / down one step while maintaining other supplied values.
If the field being bruteforced is not provided, it will iterate through the full range while maintaining other supplied values.

options.description: delay betweens attempts in ms. (def is 1000)
direction to increment field value. (def is both directions)
direction to decrement field value. (def is both directions)

lf_t55xx_clonehelp.description: Display a list of available commands for cloning specific techs on T5xx tags

lf_t55xx_config.description:
Set/Get T55XX configuration of the pm3 client. Like modulation, inverted, offset, rate etc.
Offset is start position to decode data.

options.description: set/reset as Q5/T5555 chip instead of T55x7
set configuration from a block0 (4 hex bytes)
set offset, where data should start decode in bitstream

examples: lf t55xx config --FSK -i            -> FSK demodulation, inverse data
lf t55xx config --FSK -i -o 3       -> FSK demodulation, inverse data, offset 3

lf_t55xx_dangerraw.description:
This command allows to emit arbitrary raw commands on T5577 and cut the field after arbitrary duration.
Uncontrolled usage can easily write an invalid configuration, activate lock bits,
OTP bit, password protection bit, deactivate test-mode, lock your card forever.
WARNING: this may lock definitively the tag in an unusable state!

options.description: <0 - 200000> time in microseconds before dropping the field

lf_t55xx_detect.description: Try detecting the tag modulation from reading the configuration block

lf_t55xx_deviceconfig.description:
Sets t55x7 timings for direct commands.
The timings are set here in Field Clocks (FC) which is converted to (US) on device.

options.description: Set write TWO gap (1 of 4 only)
Set write THREE gap (1 of 4 only)
Set default t55x7 timings (use `-p` to save if required)

lf_t55xx_dump.description:
This command dumps a T55xx card Page 0 block 0-7.
It will create two files (bin/json)

options.description: override, force pwd read despite danger to card

lf_t55xx_info.description:
Show T55x7 configuration data (page 0/ blk 0) from reading the configuration block
from tag. Use `-c` to specify a config block data to be used instead of reading tag.

options.description: use these data instead (4 hex bytes)
interprete provided data as T5555/Q5 config

lf_t55xx_p1detect.description: Detect Page 1 of a T55xx chip

lf_t55xx_read.description:
Read T55xx block data.  This commands defaults to page 0.
* * * WARNING * * *
Use of read with password on a tag not configured
for a password can damage the tag
* * * * * * * * * *
examples: lf t55xx read -b 0                         -> read data from block 0
lf t55xx read -b 0 --pwd 01020304          -> read data from block 0, pwd 01020304
lf t55xx read -b 0 --pwd 01020304 -o       -> read data from block 0, pwd 01020304, override

lf_t55xx_resetread.description:
Send Reset Cmd then `lf read` the stream to attempt
to identify the start of it (needs a demod and/or plot after)

lf_t55xx_restore.description: Restore T55xx card page 0/1 n blocks from (bin/eml/json) dump file

options.description: Specify a filename for dump file
password if target card has password set (4 hex bytes)

lf_t55xx_trace.description: Show T55x7 configuration data (page 0/ blk 0) from reading the configuration block

lf_t55xx_wakeup.description: This commands sends the Answer-On-Request command and leaves the readerfield ON afterwards
examples: lf t55xx wakeup -p 11223344         -> send wakeup with password

options.description: Specify a filename for dump file

examples: lf t55xx write -b 3 -d 11223344                               -> write 11223344 to block 3
lf t55xx write -b 3 -d 11223344 --pwd 01020304                -> write 11223344 to block 3, pwd 01020304
lf t55xx write -b 3 -d 11223344 --pwd 01020304 --verify       -> write 11223344 to block 3 and try validating write

lf_t55xx_bruteforce.description:
This command uses bruteforce to scan a number range.
Try reading Page 0, block 7 before.
WARNING this may brick non-password protected chips!

lf_t55xx_chk.description:
This command uses a dictionary attack.
For some cloners, try '--em' for known pwdgen algo.
Try to reading Page 0 block 7 before.
WARNING: this may brick non-password protected chips!
examples: lf t55xx chk -m                          -> use dictionary from flash memory (RDV4)
lf t55xx chk -f my_dictionary_pwds       -> loads a default keys dictionary file
lf t55xx chk --em aa11223344             -> try known pwdgen algo from some cloners based on EM4100 ID

lf_t55xx_protect.description:
This command sets the pwd bit on T5577.
WARNING this locks the tag!
examples: lf t55xx protect -n 01020304                   -> sets new pwd 01020304
lf t55xx protect -p 11223344 -n 00000000       -> use pwd 11223344, sets new pwd 00000000

lf_t55xx_recoverpw.description:
This command uses a few tricks to try to recover mangled password.
Try reading Page 0, block 7 before.
WARNING this may brick non-password protected chips!

lf_t55xx_sniff.description:
Sniff LF t55xx based trafic and decode possible cmd / blocks.
Lower tolerance means tighter pulses.

options.description: set samples width for ONE pulse (default auto)
set samples width for ZERO pulse (default auto)

examples: lf t55xx sniff -1 -t 2                    -> use buffer with tolerance of 2
lf t55xx sniff -1 --zero 7 --one 14       -> use buffer, zero pulse width 7, one pulse width 15

lf_t55xx_special.description: Show block changes with 64 different offsets,  data taken from DemodBuffer.

lf_t55xx_wipe.description: This commands wipes a tag, fills blocks 1-7 with zeros and a default configuration block

options.description: specify writing to Q5/T5555 tag using dedicated config block

examples: lf t55xx wipe                  -> wipes a T55x7 tag, config block 0x000880E0
lf t55xx wipe --q5             -> wipes a Q5/T5555 tag, config block 0x6001F004
lf t55xx wipe -p 11223344      -> wipes a T55x7 tag, config block 0x000880E0, using pwd

mem_baudrate.description:
Set the baudrate for the SPI flash memory communications.
Reading Flash ID will virtually always fail under 48MHz setting.
Unless you know what you are doing, please stay at 24MHz.
If >= 24MHz, FASTREADS instead of READS instruction will be used.

mem_dump.description: Dumps flash memory on device into a file or view in console
examples: mem dump -f myfile                                -> download all flashmem to file
mem dump --view -o 262015 --len 128               -> display 128 bytes from offset 262015 (RSA sig)
mem dump --view -f myfile -o 241664 --len 58      -> display 58 bytes from offset 241664 and save to file

mem_info.description: Collect signature and verify it from flash memory
examples: mem info -s -p pm3_generic_private_key.pem           -> generate a RSA 1024 signature for your SPI ID
mem info -s -p pm3_generic_private_key.pem -w        -> generate and write a RSA 1024 signature for your SPI ID

mem_load.description:
Loads binary file into flash memory on device
Warning! - mem area to be written must have been wiped first
OBS! - dictionaries are serviced as files in spiffs so no wipe is needed
examples: mem load -f myfile                         -> upload file myfile values at default offset 0
mem load -f myfile -o 1024                 -> upload file myfile values at offset 1024
mem load -f mfc_default_keys -m            -> upload MIFARE Classic keys
mem load -f t55xx_default_pwds -t          -> upload T55XX passwords
mem load -f iclass_default_keys -i         -> upload iCLASS keys
mem load -f mfulc_default_keys --ulc       -> upload MIFARE UL-C keys

mem_wipe.description:
Wipe flash memory on device, which fills it with 0xFF
[ !!! OBS ] use with caution

mem_spiffs_copy.description: Copy a file to another (destructively) in SPIFFS file system

mem_spiffs_check.description: Check/try to defrag faulty/fragmented SPIFFS file system

mem_spiffs_dump.description:
Dumps device SPIFFS file to a local file
Size is handled by first sending a STAT command against file to verify existence
examples: mem spiffs dump -s tag.bin                 -> download binary file from device, saved as `tag.bin`
mem spiffs dump -s tag.bin -d a001         -> download tag.bin, save as `a001.bin`
mem spiffs dump -s tag.bin -t              -> download tag.bin into trace buffer

mem_spiffs_info.description: Print file system info and usage statistics

mem_spiffs_mount.description: Mount the SPIFFS file system if not already mounted

mem_spiffs_remove.description: Remove a file from SPIFFS filesystem

mem_spiffs_rename.description: Rename/move a file from SPIFFS filesystem.

mem_spiffs_test.description: Test SPIFFS Operations, require wiping pages 0 and 1

mem_spiffs_tree.description: Print the Flash memory file system tree

mem_spiffs_upload.description:
Uploads binary-wise file into device file system
Warning: mem area to be written must have been wiped first.
This is already taken care when loading dictionaries.
File names can only be 32 bytes long on device SPIFFS

mem_spiffs_view.description: View a file on flash memory on device in console

mem_spiffs_wipe.description:
* * *  Warning  * * *
This command wipes all files on the device SPIFFS file system

mqtt_send.description:
This command send MQTT messages.  You can send JSON file
Default server:  mqtt.proxdump.com:1883  topic: proxdump
examples: mqtt send --msg "Hello from Pm3"           -> sending msg to default server/port/topic
mqtt send -f myfile.json                     -> sending file to default server/port/topic
mqtt send --addr test.mosquitto.org -p 1883 --topic pm3 --msg "custom mqtt server "

mqtt_receive.description:
This command receives MQTT messages.  JSON text will be saved to file if detected
Default server:  proxdump.com:1883  topic: proxdump

options.description: file name to use for received files

examples: mqtt receive                   -> listening to default server/port/topic

nfc_decode.description:
Decode and print NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF)
You must provide either data in hex or a filename, but not both

nfc_type2_read.usage: hf mfu ndefread [-hlv] [-k Replace default key for NDEF] [-f <fn>] [--schann]

options.description: use secure channel. Must have key

nfc_type4a_format.description: Format ISO14443-a Tag as a NFC tag with Data Exchange Format (NDEF)

nfc_type4a_read.description: Read NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF) file on Type 4 NDEF tag

nfc_type4a_write.description: Write raw NDEF hex bytes to tag. This commands assumes tag already been NFC/NDEF formatted.

options.description: write raw NDEF file to tag
fix NDEF record headers / terminator block if missing

nfc_type4a_st25taread.description: Read NFC Data Exchange Format (NDEF) file on ST25TA

nfc_mf_cformat.description:
format MIFARE Classic Tag as a NFC tag with Data Exchange Format (NDEF)
If no <name> given, UID will be used as filename.
It will try default keys and MAD keys to detect if tag is already formatted in order to write.
If not, it will try finding a key file based on your UID.  ie, if you ran autopwn before

options.description: use key B for access sectors (by default: key A)

nfc_mf_cwrite.description: Write raw NDEF hex bytes to tag. This commands assumes tag already been NFC/NDEF formatted.

options.description: write raw NDEF file to tag
fix NDEF record headers / terminator block if missing
use key B for access sectors (by default: key A)
raw, provided bytes should include CRC

options.flag: -w, -W, --wired                Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

options.description: Applet ID to select. By default A0000003080000100 will be used

examples: piv select -s                  -> select card, select applet
piv select -st --aid a00000030800001000        -> select card, select applet a00000030800001000, show result in TLV

piv_getdata.description: Get a data container of a given tag

options.flag: -w, -W, --wired                Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

options.description: Applet ID to select. By default A0000003080000100 will be used

options.flag: <hex>                          Tag ID to read, between 1 and 3 bytes.

examples: piv getdata -s 5fc102          -> select card, select applet, get card holder unique identifer
piv getdata -st 5fc102         -> select card, select applet, get card holder unique identifer, show result in TLV

piv_authsign.description: Send a nonce and ask the PIV card to sign it

options.flag: -w, -W, --wired                Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

options.description: Applet ID to select. By default A0000003080000100 will be used
Slot number. Default will be 0x9E (card auth cert).
Algorithm to use to sign. Example values: 06=RSA-1024, 07=RSA-2048, 17=ECC-P256 (default), 20=ECC-P384

examples: piv sign -sk                   -> select card, select applet, sign a NULL nonce

piv_scan.description: Scan a PIV card for known containers

options.flag: -w, -W, --wired                Send data via contact (iso7816) interface. (def: Contactless interface)

options.description: Applet ID to select. By default A0000003080000100 will be used

examples: piv scan -s                    -> select card, select applet and run scan
piv scan -st --aid a00000030800001000        -> select card, select applet a00000030800001000, show result of the scan in TLV

piv_list.description:
Alias of `trace list -t 7816` with selected protocol data to annotate trace buffer
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default
It accepts all other arguments of `trace list`. Note that some might not be relevant for this specific protocol

options.description: display times in microseconds instead of clock cycles
show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

options.flag: or to import into Wireshark using encapsulation type "ISO 14443"

examples: piv list --frame               -> show frame delay times

smart_list.description:
Alias of `trace list -t 7816` with selected protocol data to annotate trace buffer
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default
It accepts all other arguments of `trace list`. Note that some might not be relevant for this specific protocol

options.description: display times in microseconds instead of clock cycles
show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

options.flag: or to import into Wireshark using encapsulation type "ISO 14443"

examples: smart list --frame             -> show frame delay times

smart_brute.description: Tries to bruteforce SFI, using a known list of AID's

options.description: executes TLV decoder if it possible

smart_info.description: Extract more detailed information from smart card.

smart_pcsc.description: Make pm3 available to host OS smartcard driver via vpcd to enable use with other software such as GlobalPlatform Pro

options.description: display APDU transactions between OS and card

smart_reader.description: Act as a smart card reader.

options.description: active smartcard without select (reset sc module)
executes TLV decoder if it possible
Timeout in MS waiting for SIM to respond. (def 337ms)

examples: smart raw -s -0 -d 00a404000e315041592e5359532e4444463031       -> `1PAY.SYS.DDF01` PPSE directory with get ATR

smart_upgrade.description:
[=] --------------------------------------------------------------------
[!] WARNING - sim module firmware upgrade
[!] A dangerous command, do wrong and you could brick the sim module
[=] --------------------------------------------------------------------
Upgrade RDV4 sim module firmware

smart_setclock.description: Set clock speed for smart card interface.

script_list.description: List available Lua, Cmd and Python scripts

script_run.description:
Run a Lua, Cmd or Python script. If no extension it will search for lua/cmd/py extensions
Use `script list` to see available scripts

trace_extract.description: Extracts protocol authentication challenges from trace buffer

trace_list.description:
Annotate trace buffer with selected protocol data
You can load a trace from file (see `trace load -h`) or it be downloaded from device by default

options.description: display times in microseconds instead of clock cycles
show hexdump to convert to pcap(ng)

options.flag: or to import into Wireshark using encapsulation type "ISO 14443"

examples: trace list -t raw              -> just show raw data without annotations
trace list -t cryptorf         -> interpret as CryptoRF
trace list -t des              -> interpret as MIFARE DESFire
trace list -t felica           -> interpret as ISO18092 / FeliCa
trace list -t ht1              -> interpret as Hitag 1
trace list -t ht2              -> interpret as Hitag 2
trace list -t hts              -> interpret as Hitag S
trace list -t htu              -> interpret as Hitag µ
trace list -t iclass           -> interpret as iCLASS
trace list -t legic            -> interpret as LEGIC
trace list -t lto              -> interpret as LTO-CM
trace list -t mf               -> interpret as MIFARE Classic and decrypt crypto1 stream
trace list -t seos             -> interpret as SEOS
trace list -t thinfilm         -> interpret as Thinfilm
trace list -t topaz            -> interpret as Topaz
trace list -t mfp              -> interpret as MIFARE Plus
trace list -t fmcos20          -> interpret as FMCOS 2.0
trace list -t mf -f mfc_default_keys.dic          -> use default dictionary file
trace list -t 14a --frame                         -> show frame delay times

trace_load.description:
Load protocol data from binary file to trace buffer
File extension is <.trace>
examples: trace load -f mytracefile      -> w/o file extension

trace_save.description:
Save protocol data from trace buffer to binary file
File extension is <.trace>
examples: trace save -f mytracefile      -> w/o file extension

usart_btpin.description:
Change BT add-on PIN.
WARNING: this requires
1) BTpower to be turned ON
2) BT add-on to NOT be connected
=> the add-on blue LED must blink

usart_btfactory.description:
Reset BT add-on to factory settings
This requires
1) BTpower to be turned ON
2) BT add-on to NOT be connected
=> the add-on blue LED must blink
WARNING: process only if strictly needed!

usart_tx.description:
Send string over USART.
WARNING:  it will have side-effects if used in USART HOST mode!

usart_rx.description:
Receive string over USART.
WARNING: it will have side-effects if used in USART HOST mode!

usart_txrx.description:
Send string over USART and wait for response.
WARNING: if used in USART HOST mode, you can only send AT commands
to add-on when BT connection is not established (LED needs to be blinking)
Any other usage in USART HOST mode will have side-effects!

options.description: timeout in ms, default is 1000 ms

examples: usart txrx -d "AT+VERSION"                    -> Talking to BT add-on (when no connection)
usart txrx -t 2000 -d "AT+SOMESTUFF\r\n"      -> Talking to a target requiring longer time and end-of-line chars

usart_txhex.description:
Send bytes over USART.
WARNING:  it will have side-effects if used in USART HOST mode!

usart_rxhex.description:
Receive bytes over USART.
WARNING: it will have side-effects if used in USART HOST mode!

usart_config.description:
Configure USART.
WARNING: it will have side-effects if used in USART HOST mode!
The changes are not permanent, restart Proxmark3 to get default settings back.

wiegand_encode.description: Encode wiegand formatted number to raw hex

options.description: encode to new ASN.1 encoded format
add HID ProxII preamble to wiegand output

examples: wiegand encode --bin 1                               ->  raw wiegand hex with sentinel
wiegand encode -w H10301 --fc 123 --cn 4567 --new      -> new ASN.1 encoded format

wiegand_decode.description: Decode raw hex or binary to wiegand format

options.description: new ASN.1 encoded data as raw hex to be decoded
skip preabmle checking, brute force all possible lengths for raw hex input

examples: wiegand decode --new 06BD88EB80        -> 4..13 bytes, new ASN.1 encoded format
